items.area: Direct engagement with Decision Maker (John Coyne) and Executive Sponsor (Lucy Moody)
items.quote: Hold a post-submission review call... open direct dialogue with John Coyne... Secure executive alignment with Lucy Moody

items.area: Stale Next Step language predating RFI submission
items.quote: Share the RFI primer with Sophie Onyeks and support the team in refining the draft RFI before release... Follow up with Sophie and Greig Evans once the draft is reviewed and approved for external circulation.
items.source: Next_Step__c text vs. RFI submission Task, 2026-07-10

metrics.narrative: The council has quantified scale (roughly 65 procurement officers and circa 3,000 active contracts, per MEDDPICC 2.0, CRM, 7 Jul 2026) and Sophie Onyeks named the council's annual income as '1,700,000,000' on the 22 Apr 2026 discovery call to underline the scale of the transformation, but no quantified savings, cycle-time, or ROI target has been articulated by the buyer yet. Pain is well described qualitatively; a business case in dollar or hour terms is still missing.

sources: Email thread with Sophie Onyeks, 2026-06-01 through 2026-07-10
champion.narrative: Sophie Onyeks (IT Delivery Manager) is the most consistently engaged and responsive contact across the discovery call and every clarification round from Jun-Jul 2026, coordinating the RFI internally. However, she repeatedly self-described her limited authority on the 22 Apr 2026 call ('I'm not going to be one of the big people doing the evaluations... I'm literally just running things... I'm a grunt worker'), so she reads as an engaged coordinator rather than a power-holding champion. The CRM's MEDDPICC 2.0 record (updated 2026-07-07) tentatively points to another named RFI coordinating contact within IT category management as a possible champion, but states that status is 'not yet established' — that contact is not named here per this sweep's naming policy.

competition.narrative: Salesforce's Competitors__c field is blank, and no rival vendor has been named in a Salesforce competition field or a verbatim buyer quote read this sweep. The council's own 22 Jun 2026 RFI email was addressed to 'Approved Delivery Partner, OEM and/or Systems Integrator' generally, implying a multi-vendor field, but Zycus has no confirmed visibility into which specific competitors are participating. This is a genuine knowledge gap, not a hygiene gap.

identify_pain.narrative: Sophie Onyeks gave a detailed, first-hand account on the 22 Apr 2026 call: the council runs Intend for procurement and Oracle ERP, neither of which handles contract management, so the team relies on 'a lot of Excel spreadsheets... and Sharepoint for documents.' She named the core issue as the lack of 'centralized, very good contract and solid contract management processes,' driven by best-practice and Procurement Act 2023 transformation goals, and confirmed the council cannot currently produce a risk-supplier report on demand ('That would be... too difficult').

paper_process.narrative: The framework route (GCA RM6285 Lot 2 / Back Office Software 2) is confirmed as the contracting vehicle, but the deal is still pre-award (ITT closes 7 Aug 2026) so no SOW/MSA/paper mechanics have begun. The Next Step's mutual close plan works back from the incumbent (Intent) contract's August 2027 expiry to sequence selection, contracting and implementation runway.

economic_buyer.narrative: Lucy Moody is named by Sophie Onyeks on the 22 Apr 2026 call as 'the sponsor... for this programme' and carries the title 'Assistant Director, the relevant stakeholder (Executive Sponsor)' in the Salesforce contact role; she reports into John Coyne, tagged Decision Maker and named as Procurement Director on the same call. Neither has had direct Zycus engagement yet — access runs entirely through Sophie Onyeks. The Next Step (10 Jul 2026) explicitly plans to open direct dialogue with John Coyne by 5 Aug 2026 and build executive alignment with Lucy Moody through Aug 2026, confirming this is still pending, not done.

decision_process.narrative: MEDDPICC 2.0 (CRM, 7 Jul 2026) lays out a dated phased process: RFI closed with submissions due 13 Jul 2026 (Zycus submitted 10 Jul 2026, ahead of deadline), ITT issued 13 Jul 2026 (today), ITT closes 7 Aug 2026, evaluation/award 10 Aug-4 Sep 2026, mobilisation w/c 7 Sep 2026, go-live/hypercare May 2027. On the 22 Apr 2026 call the commercial route (framework vs open market) was still undecided ('Don't know... reached out to 7 suppliers'); the subsequent RFI email confirms the route is GCA RM6285 Lot 2 (Back Office Software 2).

decision_criteria.narrative: The council's 22 Jun 2026 RFI email under GCA RM6285 Lot 2 explicitly scopes strategic sourcing, contract lifecycle management, supplier onboarding/management, procurement intake, and governance/audit workflows, with mandatory integration to the relevant stakeholder (ERP) and alignment to the UK Government Central Digital Platform. MEDDPICC 2.0 (CRM, 7 Jul 2026) confirms full AP automation/invoice-payment processing is explicitly out of scope, and flags a phased approach for requisitioning/catalogue management.

items.summary: Dear both See attached and below.See list of delivery partners on the list for the Framework we are using. Regards Sophie OFFICIAL From:Jamie Parris Sent:Monday, June 22, 2026 15:05 Cc:Sophie Onyeks Subject:Request for Information under GCA RM6285, Lot 2…

items.name: Following Up – Zycus & Birmingham City Council | Source-to-Pay
items.summary: I hope you're doing well. My name is Claire Hudson, I'm reaching out as I've recently taken over the relationship with Birmingham City Council and wanted to formally introduce myself.I've been fully briefed on the excellent introductory conversation held in…

items.name: Re: Following Up – Zycus & Birmingham City Council | Source-to-Pay
items.summary: Great to connect with you too.Yes, I'm your main point of contact going forward, though Shrika remains part of the wider team if needed, everything comes through me from here.Completely understand, leadership transitions take time to settle and it sounds like…

day_summary.overall: Most recent activity was on 10 Jul: 4 emails — Following Up – Zycus & Birmingham City Council | Source-to-Pay. Recent deal changes: Stage moved Qualified → Formal Evaluation.

cro_panel.intro: The deal is a live, buyer-driven public-sector RFI that Zycus met on deadline, but it remains single-threaded through an engaged coordinator with limited authority, with no direct line yet to the named Decision Maker or Executive Sponsor, and no confirmed visibility into the competitive field

bullets.text: Economic buyer and sponsor untouched; John Coyne (decision maker) and Lucy Moody (exec sponsor) have had zero Zycus engagement; both are only targeted for Jul/Aug outreach. ► Convert the Greig Evans introduction and Lucy Moody sponsor touch into real meetings before the shortlist forms

Champion has no authority; Sophie Onyeks is responsive across every touchpoint but has 'explicitly and repeatedly disclaimed evaluation authority.' ► Use Sophie purely as a broker to Greig Evans/John Coyne, not as the influence path
Competitive field is genuinely open; the council's RFI was addressed generally to 'Approved Delivery Partner, OEM and/or Systems Integrator,' implying multiple vendors with none named or ruled out
Commercial and stage trajectory both up; amount moved $150K→$250K and stage advanced Qualified→Formal Evaluation inside the last ~2 months, a real buyer-side signal of increasing scope/seriousness
Buyer's own process is genuinely live; the RM6285 Lot 2 RFI is active with a firm 10 Jul 2026 response deadline, not a stalled or hypothetical deal
Single-threaded account; Sophie Onyeks remains the only substantively engaged contact after 82+ days since the one recorded meeting

fit.ai_fit_signal: Sophie Onyeks confirmed on the 22 Apr 2026 call that current requirements don't formally include AI but the council is adding an RFI question specifically to assess AI capability: 'our requirements at the moment doesn't don't include AI. We
fit.expectations_fit: Zycus' proposed scope tracks closely to the council's stated RFI requirements: mandatory Oracle Cloud Fusion integration, Central Digital Platform/Procurement Act 2023 transparency alignment, and an upstream Source-to-Contract scope explici
fit.north_star_verdict: Pipeline is appropriately conservative: the council is still running its own competitive RFI/ITT under RM6285 Lot 2 with no vendor down-select yet, and Zycus has no direct engagement with the named Decision Maker (John Coyne) or Executive S
fit.positioning_strength: Zycus is deliberately leading its RFI response with Merlin/agentic AI positioning per the current Next Step, in response to the council's own stated interest in evaluating AI capability.

deal.next_step: By 10 Jul 2026: Complete and submit the written RFI response with the EY bid team, leading with Zycus’ connected S2C platform, Merlin / agentic AI, Oracle integration, contract lifecycle management, supplier risk reporting, and standardized intake story. Confirm written receipt with Sophie Onyeks and close out the RM6285 Lot 2 framework-route clarification before the response goes out. By 21 Jul 2026: Ask Sophie Onyeks to broker a direct introduction to Greig Evans so Zycus is multi-threaded to the procurement lead who reviews and approves the RFI for external release. By 5 Aug 2026: Hold a post-submission review call to confirm the RFP/demo timeline and open direct dialogue with John Coyne on procurement mandate, evaluation criteria, and decision process. Through Aug 2026: Secure executive alignment with Lucy Moody as the RFP shortlist forms and position Zycus at sponsor level around the transformation outcome. Mutual close plan: RFI → RFP/demo → shortlist → contract, working back from the August 2027 incumbent Intent contract expiry to ensure selection, contracting, and implementation runway. They have had a change in leadership which is why it has taken so long - We have had a change in leadership which has delayed the project. It should pick up at pace now, but we aren’t in a position to send out the RFI yet. I will let you know when we are. I am hoping it will be soon. We will request demos after receiving the RFI if we feel we need demos at this stage. It is something we haven’t discussed in detail with the team yet. Claire is chasing on this for a update. Share the RFI primer with Sophie Onyeks and support the team in refining the draft RFI before release, especially around AI capabilities, Oracle integration, procurement volumes, framework preference, and implementation model. Follow up with Sophie and Greig Evans once the draft is reviewed and approved for external circulation.

We're adding a question in there to see what kind of capabilities you've got.'

business_case.evidence: Pain is clearly and repeatedly articulated (fragmented systems, manual Excel/SharePoint contract management, inability to report on risk suppliers), but no quantified savings, ROI, or efficiency target has been stated by the buyer (Avoma call, 2026-04-22; MEDDPICC 2.0, CRM, 2026-07-07).

items.change: New Zycus account owner (Claire Hudson) introduced herself to Sophie Onyeks, replacing the prior contact
Council issued the formal RFI under GCA RM6285 Lot 2, scoping an upstream Source-to-Contract platform integrating with Oracle Cloud Fusion and the UK Central Digital Platform
Zycus sent RFI clarification questions to the council as a tracked spreadsheet
Council confirmed submission channel is email, not the In-tend portal
Zycus submitted its written RFI response ahead of the 13 Jul 2026 deadline and asked for the outcome-notification timeline

deal_movement.summary: Since the 22 Apr 2026 discovery call, the relationship has moved entirely through email: a new account owner introduction (1 Jun 2026), the formal RFI issuance (22 Jun 2026), two rounds of written clarification questions (26 and 29 Jun 2026), submission-channel confirmation (7 Jul 2026), and the RFI response submission ahead of deadline (10 Jul 2026).

forecast_read.math: 283 days to close (13 Jul 2026 -> 22 Apr 2027). Opportunity created 2026-04-22, so ~82 days in the pipeline, all inside Formal Evaluation. RFI submitted 10 Jul 2026, ITT issued 13 Jul 2026 (today), ITT closes 7 Aug 2026, evaluation/award 10 Aug-4 Sep 2026, mobilisation w/c 7 Sep 2026, go-live/hypercare May 2027 (MEDDPICC 2.0, CRM, 7 Jul 2026). Recorded close date sits ~7-8 months after the stated award window but roughly aligns with the incumbent contract's August 2027 expiry (Next Step, 10 Jul 2026) plus implementation runway to the May 2027 go-live target — worth a rep reconciliation but not indefensible.
forecast_read.reason: Pipeline is appropriately conservative: the council is still running its own competitive RFI/ITT under RM6285 Lot 2 with no vendor down-select yet, and Zycus has no direct engagement with the named Decision Maker (John Coyne) or Executive Sponsor (Lucy Moody). The long close-date runway is consistent with the CRM's own decision-process timeline (evaluation/award 10 Aug-4 Sep 2026, mobilisation Sep 2026, go-live/hypercare May 2027) bridging to the incumbent Intent contract's August 2027 expiry cited in the Next Step, rather than an over-optimistic date.

Decision Maker not yet engaged — John Coyne (Procurement Director, tagged Decision Maker) has been named since the 22 Apr 2026 call but has had no direct Zycus contact; the deal remains single-threaded through Sophie Onyeks.

Competitive field unconfirmed — Competitors__c is blank and no rival vendor has been named in a buyer quote or CRM field this sweep, despite the RFI being addressed to multiple approved delivery partners (22 Jun 2026 email).

deal_momentum.text: Two-way email engagement inside the last week — the buyer confirmed the RFI submission channel by email on 7 Jul 2026 ('Correct' — Sophie Onyeks), a genuine buyer reply.

Live, dated ITT process — ITT issued 13 Jul 2026 (today), closes 7 Aug 2026, award expected 10 Aug-4 Sep 2026 per MEDDPICC 2.0, so the immediate post-submission quiet period is expected process cadence, not a stall.
Outreach outstanding a reply — Claire Hudson's 10 Jul 2026 email asking for the outcome-notification timeline has not yet been answered as of 13 Jul 2026 (3 days).

customer_commitment.text: Buyer-driven RFI process — the council initiated and is running its own formal RFI/ITT under RM6285 Lot 2, with Sophie Onyeks actively managing clarification rounds through Jun-Jul 2026.
No buyer-voiced preference — nothing in the evidence read has the council expressing a preference for Zycus over other bidders; standing is unconfirmed.

items.quote: Out of scope: full AP automation, invoice/payment processing.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus' proposed scope tracks closely to the council's stated RFI requirements: mandatory Oracle Cloud Fusion integration, Central Digital Platform/Procurement Act 2023 transparency alignment, and an upstream Source-to-Contract scope explicitly excluding full AP automation and invoice/payment processing.

hard.next_step: By 10 Jul 2026: Complete and submit the written RFI response with the EY bid team, leading with Zycus’ connected S2C platform, Merlin / agentic AI, Oracle integration, contract lifecycle management, supplier risk reporting, and standardized intake story. Confirm written receipt with Sophie Onyeks and close out the RM6285 Lot 2 framework-route clarification before the response goes out. By 21 Jul 2026: Ask Sophie Onyeks to broker a direct introduction to Greig Evans so Zycus is multi-threaded to the procurement lead who reviews and approves the RFI for external release. By 5 Aug 2026: Hold a post-submission review call to confirm the RFP/demo timeline and open direct dialogue with John Coyne on procurement mandate, evaluation criteria, and decision process. Through Aug 2026: Secure executive alignment with Lucy Moody as the RFP shortlist forms and position Zycus at sponsor level around the transformation outcome. Mutual close plan: RFI → RFP/demo → shortlist → contract, working back from the August 2027 incumbent Intent contract expiry to ensure selection, contracting, and implementation runway. They have had a change in leadership which is why it has taken so long - <span style="color: inherit; font-family: inherit;">We have had a change in leadership which has delayed the project. It should pick up at pace now, but we aren’t in a position to send out the RFI yet. I will let you know when we are. I am hoping it will be soon. <span style="color: inherit; font-family: inherit;"> <span style="color: inherit; font-family: inherit;">We will request demos after receiving the RFI if we feel we need demos at this stage. It is something we haven’t discussed in detail with the team yet. <span style="color: inherit; font-family: inherit;"> Claire is chasing on this for a update. Share the RFI primer with Sophie Onyeks and support the team in refining the draft RFI before release, especially around AI capabilities, Oracle integration, procurement volumes, framework preference, and implementation model. Follow up with Sophie and Greig Evans once the draft is reviewed and approved for external circulation.
